Handees

Privacy Policy

Last Updated: March 2026

1. Who We Are

Handees acts as the "Data Controller" under the Nigeria Data Protection Act (NDPA) 2023. This means we determine how and why your personal data is processed.

2. The Data We Collect

To maintain a secure network of elite artisans, we collect:

  • Standard Personal Data: Full name, phone number, residential address, and trade experience.
  • Sensitive Personal Data: Your National Identity Number (NIN) and a 10-second facial verification video (biometric data).
  • Third-Party Data: Name, phone number, and address of your guarantor.

3. Our Lawful Basis for Processing

We process your Standard Personal Data based on our Legitimate Interest to operate a secure marketplace and facilitate contracts between you and clients. We process your Sensitive Personal Data (NIN and video) strictly on the basis of your Explicit Consent, obtained during onboarding, to verify your identity against the NIMC database and prevent fraud.

4. How We Share Your Data

  • Clients: If you are matched for a job, your verified name, trade, and phone number will be shared with the client. Your NIN and home address are never shared with clients.
  • Verification Partners: Your NIN is transmitted securely to licensed third-party aggregators solely to query the NIMC database.
  • Authorities: We will disclose your information to Nigerian law enforcement if formally required by law or to investigate severe fraud.

5. Data Retention

  • Approved Artisans: Your data is securely stored on encrypted servers for as long as your account is active.
  • Rejected Applications: If your application is rejected, your verification video and uploaded documents are permanently deleted from our servers within 30 days.

6. Your Rights Under the NDPA 2023

You have the right to:

  • Request access to the personal data we hold about you.
  • Request correction of inaccurate data.
  • Withdraw your consent and request the deletion of your account and data ("Right to be Forgotten").
  • Lodge a formal complaint with the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) if you believe your data has been mishandled.

To exercise these rights, contact our Data Protection team via our official WhatsApp support channel.
